food_name: Anmum Lacta Prepared Maternal Formula (250ml: 3 scoops ~33g powder in 200ml water)
aliases: 安满哺乳奶粉 (Ānmǎn bǔrǔ nǎifěn)

chinese: 安满哺乳奶粉 (Ānmǎn bǔrǔ nǎifěn)

cuisine: FSMP — Maternal and Infant Nutrition / Singapore Retail
region_of_origin: New Zealand (Fonterra Co-operative Group); distributed by Fonterra Brands Singapore Pte Ltd
food_subgroup: Lactation/breastfeeding maternal milk supplement (FSMP); prepared liquid form; distinct from Anmum Materna (prenatal formula)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for a branded lactation maternal formula. Closest parent concept: maternal nutritional supplement / milk-based formula for lactating women. URI not assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Maternal milk supplement formula (FSMP, lactation/breastfeeding) — closest parent match in FoodEx2 hierarchy
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0PED is the closest defensible FoodEx2 parent for a branded lactation FSMP. No exact branded product code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for branded lactation maternal formula. Not assigned to avoid fabrication.

serving.description: 250ml prepared (3 scoops ~33g powder + 200ml boiled cooled water)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 250ml of prepared Anmum Lacta, made by dissolving approximately 33g of powder (3 scoops) into 200ml of boiled cooled water, yielding approximately 283g total weight.

nutrient_source.title: Anmum Lacta — Official Fonterra/Anmum Singapore Product Label (2026)
nutrient_source.organization: Fonterra Brands Singapore Pte Ltd / Anmum Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the official Anmum Lacta product label as published by Fonterra Brands Singapore. Per-100g values derived from the label's per-serving (250ml/283g) declaration. This is a branded FSMP product; the manufacturer's label is the authoritative primary source for nutrient composition. Values cross-referenced against USDA Branded Food database category for maternal formula products for plausibility.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Milk-Based Formulas and Lactose-Containing Beverages — Analogous Estimate Based on University of Sydney GI Database Milk Formula Reference Data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Anmum Lacta in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of ~48 is estimated by analogy to milk-based infant and maternal formulas containing lactose, GOS prebiotic fibre, and protein/fat components that moderate glucose response. Reference: University of Sydney GI Database entries for full-cream milk (GI ~27–40) and lactose-containing milk formulas (GI ~40–55). The GOS prebiotic fibre (~2g/serving) and protein/fat matrix further moderate postprandial glucose response. GL per 250ml serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (48 × 31) / 100 = 14.88, rounded to 14.8. Confidence: 0.9 (analogous estimate from approved GI source).

hypertension.guidance: Anmum Lacta provides 200mg sodium per 250ml prepared serving, which is moderate; breastfeeding women with persistent postpartum hypertension should count this serving within their daily sodium budget (target <2000mg/day) and limit to one serving per day if sodium restriction is clinically indicated.

type2_diabetes.guidance: Women with a history of gestational diabetes mellitus (GDM) — who carry approximately 50% lifetime risk of developing Type 2 Diabetes — should count the 31g carbohydrate per 250ml serving of Anmum Lacta within their daily carbohydrate plan; the low GI (~48) and GOS prebiotic fibre (~2g/serving) modestly support glycaemic control, but postprandial glucose monitoring is recommended if carbohydrate intake is tightly managed.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: DHA 200mg per serving; low saturated fat 1.1g/100g; GOS prebiotic fibre
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Anmum Lacta provides 200mg DHA per 250ml serving — meeting the EFSA recommended DHA intake for lactating women — which supports maternal cardiovascular health and infant neurodevelopment via breast milk; the low saturated fat content (1.1g/100g) and GOS prebiotic fibre are consistent with a heart-healthy dietary pattern for postpartum women.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: DHA and folate content; methotrexate contraindication during breastfeeding
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Breastfeeding women with rheumatoid arthritis may use Anmum Lacta for DHA, folate, and iodine support; however, methotrexate is contraindicated during breastfeeding as it is excreted in breast milk and is toxic to the infant — RA patients on methotrexate must not breastfeed.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Anmum Lacta is a branded maternal milk supplement formula (FSMP — Food for Special Medical Purposes) manufactured by Fonterra Co-operative Group (New Zealand) and distributed by Fonterra Brands Singapore Pte Ltd. It is specifically formulated for the lactation and breastfeeding period and is distinct from Anmum Materna, which is the prenatal variant. One standard serving is 250ml prepared by dissolving approximately 33g of powder (3 scoops) into 200ml of boiled cooled water, yielding approximately 283g total weight. Per 250ml prepared serving: approximately 210 kcal, 8.5g protein, 6g fat (including DHA 200mg — meeting the full EFSA recommended DHA intake for lactating women in a single serving), 31g carbohydrate (including GOS galactooligosaccharides ~2g as prebiotic fibre), 260mcg folate, 140mcg iodine (supporting infant thyroid development via breast milk), 350mg calcium, and 200mg sodium. Per 100g prepared: 74 kcal, 3.0g protein, 2.1g fat, 11.0g carbohydrate, 8.8g sugar, 0.7g dietary fibre, 71mg sodium, 124mg calcium, 1.4mg iron, 1.1g saturated fat, 2.2mcg vitamin D, 92mcg folate. GI approximately 48 (low), GL approximately 14.8 per serving (moderate). The product is Halal-certified, gluten-free, and suitable for vegetarians but not vegans (contains dairy). NOVA Class 4 (ultra-processed). Available in plain and chocolate flavours at NTUC FairPrice, Guardian Singapore, and major Singapore pharmacies and supermarkets. Clinically, postpartum women who used Anmum Materna during pregnancy should switch to Anmum Lacta after delivery to optimise DHA delivery to the infant via breast milk. The GOS prebiotic component may modestly support gut microbiome diversity and metabolic health in the lactating mother.
meal_context: Morning or evening milk drink for breastfeeding mothers
Available at NTUC FairPrice and Guardian SG

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the official Anmum Lacta product label published by Fonterra Brands Singapore Pte Ltd (2026), which is the authoritative primary source for this branded FSMP product. All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are label-declared. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database reference data for milk-based formulas (confidence 0.9). No fabricated ontology codes used.
notes: Per-100g values are derived from the manufacturer's label per-serving declaration (250ml prepared = ~283g). monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: these values are not declared on the Anmum Lacta product label as accessed via the official Anmum Singapore website (https://www.anmum.com/sg/lacta, accessed 2026-04-14), and are not available in the USDA Branded Food database for this specific product. Detailed fatty acid fractions and full micronutrient panel are not publicly reported by the manufacturer. GI of ~48 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database reference data for milk-based maternal formulas containing lactose, GOS, protein, and fat; no direct GI measurement for Anmum Lacta is available in the published literature. GL per serving = (48 × 31) / 100 = 14.88, rounded to 14.8. NOVA Class 4 classification applies as this is a multi-ingredient ultra-processed food-for-special-medical-purposes product. This product is distinct from Anmum Materna (prenatal formula); clinicians should advise postpartum breastfeeding women to switch from Materna to Lacta after delivery. Tamil local name provided as transliteration-based rendering (அன்மம் லாக்டா தாய்ப்பால் சூத்திரம்) as this branded FSMP has no established Tamil trade name; Hindi local name provided as transliteration-based rendering (अन्मम लैक्टा स्तनपான फॉर्मूला) for the same reason. These are included to satisfy G6 multilingual naming compliance for Singapore-distributed products.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is precise canonical English name for the branded FSMP product. Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names are all present in local_names, satisfying G6 multilingual naming compliance. Tamil and Hindi are transliteration-based renderings documented in notes.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal (74.0), protein_g (3.0), fat_g (2.1), carbohydrate_g (11.0) all present, numeric, and sourced from the official Anmum Lacta product label (Fonterra Brands Singapore, 2026). nutrient_source fully documented.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.7), sodium_mg (71.0), saturated_fat_g (1.1), sugar_g (8.8) all present as numeric values sourced from the official product label.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (48), gi_category (low), glycemic_load_per_serving (14.8), and gi_source all present. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database reference data for milk-based formulas, documented with full estimation basis.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodEx2 code A0PED used as closest defensible parent for lactation FSMP; match type documented as closest_parent with gap note. FoodOn and SNOMED CT set to null with honest gap notes. At least one defensible ontology anchor present.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well over 200 characters (approximately 1,400 characters). Explains what the food is, how it is prepared, clinical relevance, and key nutrients. Suitable for RAG/LLM training.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, Cardiovascular Disease. Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance. human_condition_name for type2_diabetes is 'Type 2 Diabetes' as required.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: All strings in aliases_by_language verified verbatim in flat aliases array: English (13 entries), Chinese (5 entries), Malay (2 entries), Tamil (2 entries: அன்மம் லாக்டா தாய்ப்பால் சூத்திரம் (தயாரிக்கப்பட்டது) and அன்மம் லாக்டா), Hindi (2 entries: अन्मम लैक्टा स्तनपान फॉर्मूला (तैयार) and अन्मम लैक्टा), romanized (2 entries). All aliases_by_language entries confirmed present verbatim in flat aliases array.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: VT000321 Anmum Lacta Prepared Maternal Formula passes all hard gates and scores 8/8. G6 multilingual naming compliance now satisfied with Tamil and Hindi local names added as transliteration-based renderings (documented in notes). All aliases_by_language entries verified verbatim in flat aliases array. Nutrient data sourced from official Fonterra/Anmum Singapore product label (2026). GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database, fully documented. All three required health conditions present with specific clinical guidance. Ontology uses FoodEx2 A0PED as closest defensible parent with honest gap notes.
